volumeTitle: The Woman of Andros. The Self-Tormentor. The Eunuch
label: The Woman of Andros (Section 5.4.947b - Section 5.6.981b)

sections.label: Section 5.4.947b
sections.translation: So may the Gods bless me , I do believe it .

sections.label: Section 5.4.948
sections.original: Iam dudum res reduxit me ipsa in gratiam .
sections.translation: The event itself has quite brought me to reconcilement .

sections.label: Section 5.4.948b
sections.translation: O kind father ! With regard to her as a wife , since I have taken possession of her , Chremes will not offer any opposition .

sections.label: Section 5.4.949b
sections.translation: The plea is a very good one , unless perchance your father says any thing to the contrary .

sections.label: Section 5.4.950b
sections.translation: Of course , I agree .

sections.label: Section 5.4.950c
sections.translation: Then be it so . [*]

sections.label: Section 5.4.950d
sections.translation: Her portion , Pamphilus , is ten talents .

sections.label: Section 5.4.951b
sections.translation: I alm satisfied .

sections.label: Section 5.4.951c
sections.original: Propero ad filiam . Eho mecum Crito :
sections.translation: I ’ ll hasten to my daughter . Come now , ( beckoning .) along with me , Crito ; for I suppose that she will not know me .

sections.label: Section 5.4.952b
sections.original: Cur non illam huc transferri iubes ?
sections.translation: Why don ’ t you order her to be sent for hither , to our house ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.953
sections.original: Recte admones . Davo ego istuc dedam iam negoti .
sections.translation: Well thought of ; I ’ ll at once give charge of that to Davus .

sections.label: Section 5.4.953b
sections.translation: He can ’ t do it .

sections.label: Section 5.4.954
sections.translation: How so ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.954b
sections.original: Quia habet aliud magis ex sese et maius .
sections.translation: Because he has another matter that more nearly concerns himself , and of more importance .

sections.label: Section 5.4.954c
sections.translation: What , pray ?

sections.label: Section 5.4.954d
sections.translation: He is bound .

sections.label: Section 5.4.955
sections.translation: Father , he is not rightly bound . [*]

sections.label: Section 5.4.955b
sections.translation: But I ordered to that effect .

sections.label: Section 5.4.955c
sections.translation: Prithee , do order him to be set at liberty ,

sections.label: Section 5.4.956
sections.translation: Well , be it so .

sections.label: Section 5.4.956b
sections.translation: But immediately .

sections.label: Section 5.4.956c
sections.translation: I ’ m going in .

sections.label: Section 5.4.956d
sections.original: O faustum et felicem hunc diem !
sections.translation: O fortunate and happy day !

sections.label: Section 5.5.957
sections.original: Proviso quid agat Pamphilus ; atque eccum .
sections.translation: I ’ m come to see what Pamphilus is about ; and look , here he is .

sections.label: Section 5.5.957b
sections.translation: Some one perhaps might imagine that I don ’ t believe this to be true ; but now it is clear to me that it really is true . I do think that the life of the Gods is everlasting ,

sections.label: Section 5.5.960
sections.original: Quod voluptates eorum propriae sunt ; nam mihi immortalitas
sections.translation: for this reason , because their joys are their own . [*] For immortality has been obtained by me , if no sorrow interrupts this delight . But whom in particular could I wish to be now thrown in my way , for me to relate these things to ?

sections.label: Section 5.5.963
sections.translation: What means this rapture ?

sections.label: Section 5.5.963b
sections.original: Davum video : nemo est quem mallem omnium ;
sections.translation: I see Davus . There is no one in the world whom I would choose in preference ; for I am sure that he of all people will sincerely rejoice in my happiness .

sections.label: Section 5.6.965
sections.translation: Where is Pamphilus , I wonder ?

sections.label: Section 5.6.965b
sections.translation: Here he is , Davus .

sections.label: Section 5.6.965c
sections.translation: Who ’ s that ?

sections.label: Section 5.6.965d
sections.translation: ’ Tis I ,

sections.label: Section 5.6.965e
sections.translation: Pamphilus ;

sections.label: Section 5.6.966
sections.translation: you don ’ t know what has happened to me .

sections.label: Section 5.6.966b
sections.original: Certe ; sed quid mihi obtigerit scio .
sections.translation: No really ; but I know what has happened to myself .

sections.label: Section 5.6.967
sections.translation: And I too .

sections.label: Section 5.6.967b
sections.original: More hominum evenit ut quod sim nactus mali
sections.translation: It has fallen out just like human affairs in general , that you should know the mishap I have met with , before I the good that has befallen you .

sections.label: Section 5.6.969
sections.translation: My Glycerium has discovered her parents .

sections.label: Section 5.6.969b
sections.translation: O , well done !

sections.label: Section 5.6.969c
sections.translation: Hah !

sections.label: Section 5.6.970
sections.translation: Her father is an intimate friend of ours .

sections.label: Section 5.6.970b
sections.translation: Who ?

sections.label: Section 5.6.970c
sections.translation: Chremes .

sections.label: Section 5.6.970d
sections.translation: You do tell good news .

sections.label: Section 5.6.971
sections.original: Nec mora ulla est quin eam uxorem ducam .
sections.translation: And there ’ s no hinderance to my marrying her at once .

sections.label: Section 5.6.971b
sections.translation: Is he dreaming the same that he has been wishing for when awake ?

sections.label: Section 5.6.972b
sections.translation: Then about the child , Davus .

sections.label: Section 5.6.972c
sections.translation: O , say no more ; you are the only person whom the Gods favor .

sections.label: Section 5.6.973b
sections.original: Salvus sum si haec vera sunt .
sections.translation: I ’ m all right if these things are true . I ’ ll accost them .

sections.label: Section 5.6.974b
sections.original: Quis homo est ? O Charine in tempore ipso mihi advenis .
sections.translation: Who is this ? Why , Charinus , you meet me at the very nick of time .

sections.label: Section 5.6.975
sections.translation: That ’ s all right .

sections.label: Section 5.6.975b
sections.translation: Have you heard —?

sections.label: Section 5.6.975c
sections.original: Omnia : age me in tuis secundis respice .
sections.translation: Every thing ; come , in your good fortune do have some regard for me . Chremes is now at your command ; I ’ m sure that he ’ ll do every thing you wish .

sections.label: Section 5.6.977
sections.original: Memini : atque adeo longum est nos illum expectare dum exeat .
sections.translation: I ’ ll remember you ; and because it is tedious for us to wait for him until he comes out , follow me this way ; he is now in-doors at the house of Glycerium ; do you , Davus , go home ; send with all haste to remove her thence . Why are you standing there ? Why are you delaying ?

sections.label: Section 5.6.979b
sections.translation: I ’ m going . ( PAMPHILUS and CHARINUS go into the house of GLYCERIUM . DAVUS then comes forward and addresses the Audience .) Don ’ t you wait until they come out from there ; she will be betrothed within : if there is any thing else that remains , it will be transacted in-doors .

sections.label: Section 5.6.981b
sections.translation: Grant us your applause . [*]
